# Open Problems (generated)

*Generated by `traceability/scripts/build.py` from the `OPN-*` claims — do not edit by hand.*

The `OPN-*` traceability claims are the **canonical registry** of open problems. GitHub **Issues** track the *active subset* (those currently being worked) — filter [`label:open-problem`](https://github.com/jdlongmire/triadic-reality-theory/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+label%3Aopen-problem). Each such issue carries `Tracks: OPN-id`.

| ID | Tier | Confidence | Problem | Depends on |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| OPN-001 | 2 | open | Formalize Co-Admissibility (Task 0.1) | ONT-002 |
| OPN-002 | 2 | open | Actualization-Resolution Keystone | OPN-001 |
| OPN-003 | 2 | open | Derivation / Recovery of Known Physics | OPN-001 |
| OPN-004 | 2 | open | Derivation of the Born Measure | OPN-002, OPN-001 |
| OPN-005 | 2 | open | Dimensional Analysis (ontological A vs physical S) | OPN-001 |
| OPN-006 | 2 | open | Avoid Equivocation (define terms before use) | — |
| OPN-007 | 2 | open | Theology / Metaphysics Boundary Discipline | ONT-004 |

## Statements & failure conditions

### OPN-001 — Formalize Co-Admissibility (Task 0.1)

Define co-admissibility as a criterion C(constraints) → admissible set that is well-defined, computable, and measure-independent (no free entanglement measure).

*Risk if false:* The gating task. If no formalization avoids a free measure, or every one reduces to the einselection optimization, BOTH physics conjectures (PRD-001, PRD-002) lose discriminating content and the programme collapses to re-description.

### OPN-002 — Actualization-Resolution Keystone

Characterize how A resolves unsatisfiable/unresolved admissible structure: obstruction-registering (linear → GR, projective measurement-free) or best-fit projective (quadratic → modified gravity, projective measurement).

*Risk if false:* Shared root of the Born-rule (2.2) and gravity (3.1) forks. If A is best-fit with an imposed norm, the linear/GR route closes.

### OPN-003 — Derivation / Recovery of Known Physics

Reconstruct the familiar structures — Hilbert space, probability, the Born rule, gauge symmetry, relativistic spacetime, field equations, conservation laws — from the triadic foundation, in appropriate limits.

*Risk if false:* If no known physics is recovered in any limit, TRT remains ontology, not physics.

### OPN-004 — Derivation of the Born Measure

Derive the Born rule's form from constraints on actualizing action A across the admissible→actual boundary, rather than assuming it; and address the hard residue (why one admissible branch actualizes rather than another on a given occasion).

*Risk if false:* If the measure must be assumed, TRT inherits the measurement problem's hard core unchanged (as conceded).

### OPN-005 — Dimensional Analysis (ontological A vs physical S)

Develop the relation between ontological action A, the physical action functional S, energy, and time; justify the inference from the prominence of S in physics to the ontological role of A (the S→A bridge).

*Risk if false:* Without the S→A bridge, the variational apparatus and the energy-as-action-rate identification stay metaphorical.

### OPN-006 — Avoid Equivocation (define terms before use)

Logic, information, and action carry different meanings across disciplines. TRT must fix its terms before deploying them. Standing methodological discipline, not a one-time resolvable result.

*Risk if false:* Equivocation silently smuggles unearned content across the formal/physical/metaphysical boundary.

### OPN-007 — Theology / Metaphysics Boundary Discipline

Keep formal, physical, and metaphysical claims distinct; state the one explicit posit (actuality is a genuine status distinct from possibility, §9.3) openly. Disclosure is the standard, not the absence of metaphysical commitment.

*Risk if false:* An unannounced metaphysical posit operating as if it were physics would breach the methodology.

---

**Testability** (paper §12 challenge 4) is tracked as the prediction claims `PRD-001` / `PRD-002`, not as an OPN problem (it is the discriminating output, not a belt task).
